terms.definition: The right of workers to form and join organizations of their choosing without prior authorization.
The process of negotiation between employers and workers' representatives to determine conditions of employment.
All work or service exacted from any person under the menace of any penalty and for which the person has not offered themselves voluntarily.
Work that deprives children of their childhood, potential, and dignity, and that is harmful to their physical and mental development.
Any distinction, exclusion, or preference that has the effect of nullifying or impairing equality of opportunity or treatment in employment or occupation.
The process through which enterprises identify, prevent, mitigate, and account for how they address their adverse human rights impacts.

translations.fr: Devoir de vigilance en matière de droits humains

terms.definition: Any act prejudicing a worker because of union membership or participation in union activities, including dismissal, demotion, transfer, or denial of benefits.
The discipline dealing with the prevention of work-related injuries and diseases, and the protection and promotion of workers' health.

translations.es: Seguridad y salud en el trabajo

terms.definition: An agreement negotiated between a multinational enterprise and a Global Union Federation to establish a framework for labor relations across the company's global operations.
Government-established body responsible for promoting the OECD Guidelines and handling complaints (specific instances) about multinational enterprises.
The process of identifying and addressing human rights and environmental risks throughout a company's supply chain, including suppliers and subcontractors.

translations.es: Debida diligencia en la cadena de suministro
translations.fr: Devoir de vigilance dans la chaîne d'approvisionnement
translations.pt: Devida diligência na cadeia de fornecimento

terms.definition: Persons recognized as such under national law or practice, whether trade union representatives or elected representatives of the workers.
Employment that is uncertain, unpredictable, and risky from the point of view of the worker, including temporary contracts, subcontracting, and informal employment.
A formal process through which individuals or groups can raise concerns about the human rights impacts of a business and seek remedy.
A voluntary certification scheme for forest products that includes core labour requirements based on ILO fundamental conventions.
